## SQL Analysis
This project uses SQL to analyze sales data.

```sql

### Total sales (units sold) for each product.

select product_id, sum(units_sold) as total_unit_sold
from inventory_stock
group by product_id
order by total_unit_sold desc;

### Total revenue generated by each product

select product_id, sum(units_sold * price) as total_revenue_generated
from inventory_stock
group by product_id
order by total_revenue_generated desc;

### Products where inventory is less than units sold

select product_id, sum(units_sold), sum(inventory_level)
from inventory_stock
group by product_id
having sum(inventory_level) < sum(units_sold);

### Products with very high inventory but low sales

select product_id, sum(inventory_level) as Inventory, sum(units_sold) as Sales
from inventory_stock
group by product_id
having sum(inventory_level) > sum(units_sold) * 2;

### How many products fall into each inventory category?

select inventory_status, count(*) as product_count
from inventory_stock
group by inventory_status;

### How accurate is the demand forecast?

select product_id, avg(abs(demand_forecast - units_sold)) as Avg_Forecast_Error
from inventory_stock
group by product_id
order by Avg_Forecast_Error desc;

### Which products have the worst forecast accuracy?
select product_id, sum(demand_forecast - units_sold) as Total_Error
from inventory_stock
group by product_id
order by abs(Total_Error) desc
limit 5;

### Do discounts increase sales?

select discount, avg(units_sold) as Avg_Sales
from inventory_stock
group by discount
order by discount;

### Are we overpriced compared to competitors?
select 
case 
when price > competitor_pricing then 'Higher Price'
else 'Competitive or Lower'
end AS Price_Status,
avg(units_sold) as Avg_Sales
from inventory_stock
group by Price_Status;

### What are the top 3 products in each store?
select *
from (
select 
store_id,
product_id,
sum(units_sold) as Sales,
row_number() over (partition by store_id order by sum(units_sold) desc) as rn
from inventory_stock
group by store_id, product_id
) t
where rn <= 3;
